title: Immuno-biomarkers distinguish responsiveness versus non-responsiveness during immunotherapeutic treatments
abstract: A combination of biomarkers comprising a plurality of immuno-biomarkers associated with immune function that differentiates a responding patient from a non-responding one against a vaccine is provided. The combination of biomarkers comprises a plurality of immuno-biomarkers associated with immune function are three or more biomarkers selected from the group consisting of MIP-1Î±, SDF-1Î±, IL-27, IL-1Î², IL-2, IL-4, IL-5, IP-10, IL-6, IL-7, IL-8, IL-10, IL-11, Eotaxin, IL-12p70, IL-13, IL-17A, IL-31, IL-35, IL-1RA, RANTES, IFN-g, GM-CSF, TNF-a, MIP-1beta, IFN-a, MCP-1, IL-9, TNF-Î², GRO-alpha, IL-1Î±, IL-23, IL-15, IL-18, IL-21, IL-22, MIG, Granulocyte-colony stimulating factor (G-CSF or GCSF), C-X-C motif chemokine receptor 1 (CXCR1), CXCR2, CXCR3, CXCR4, CXCR5, CXCR6, CXCR7, CXCR8, CXCR9, CXCR10, CXCR11, CXCR12, CCR1, CCR2, CCR3, CCR4, CCR5, CCR6, CCR7, CCR8, CCR9, CCR10, CCL1, CCL2, CCL3, CCL4, CCL5, CCL6, CCL7, CCL8, CCL9, CCL10, CXCL1, CXCL2, CXCL3, CXCL4, CXCL5, CXCL6, CXCL7, CXCL8, CXCL9, CXCL10, CXCL11, CXCL12, CXCL13 and VEGF.
claims:
Claims 1. A combination of biomarkers comprising a plurality of immuno-biomarkers associated with immune function that differentiates a responding patient from a non- responding one against a vaccine, wherein immuno-biomarkers associated with immune function are three or more biomarkers selected from the group consisting of MIP-1 a, SDF-1 a, IL-27, ÎÎ_-1 Î², IL-2, IL-4, IL-5, IP-10, IL-6, IL-7, IL-8, IL-10, IL-1 1 , Eotaxin, IL-12p70, IL-13, IL-17A, IL-31 , IL-35, IL-1 RA, RANTES, IFN-g, GM-CSF, TNF-a, MIP-1 beta, IFN-a, MCP-1 , IL-9, TNF-Î², GRO-alpha, IL-1 a, IL-23, IL-15, IL- 18, IL-21 , IL-22, MIG, Granulocyte-colony stimulating factor (G-CSF or GCSF), C-X- C motif chemokine receptor 1 (CXCR1), CXCR2, CXCR3, CXCR4, CXCR5, CXCR6, CXCR7, CXCR8, CXCR9, CXCR10, CXCR1 1 , CXCR12, CCR1 , CCR2, CCR3, CCR4, CCR5, CCR6, CCR7, CCR8, CCR9, CCR10, CCL1 , CCL2, CCL3, CCL4, CCL5, CCL6, CCL7, CCL8, CCL9, CCL10, CXCL1 , CXCL2, CXCL3, CXCL4, CXCL5, CXCL6, CXCL7, CXCL8, CXCL9, CXCL10, CXCL1 1 , CXCL12, CXCL13 and VEGF. 2. The combination of claim 1 , wherein the vaccine is a hepatitis B vaccine. 3. The combination of claim 1 , wherein the vaccine is a chronic hepatitis B vaccine. 4. The combination of claim 1 , wherein the vaccine is an HBsAg-HBIG therapeutic vaccine (YIC). 5. The combination of claim 1 , wherein the vaccine is a hepatitis B vaccine in combination of GM-CSF. 6. The combination of claim 4, wherein the immuno-biomarkers comprise IL-10, IL-33 and MIP-1 a. 7. The combination of claim 4, wherein the immuno-biomarkers consist of IL-10, IL-33 and MIP-1 a. 8. The combination of claim 5, wherein the immuno-biomarkers either comprise IL-10, IL-13, IL-23, IFN-a, IL-18 and MIG6 or comprise IL-13, IFN-a, and IL-18. 9. The combination of claim 5, wherein the immuno-biomarkers either consist of IL-10, IL-13, IL-23, IFN-a, IL-18 and MIG6 or consist of IL-13, IFN-a, and IL-18. 10. The combination of claim 1 , wherein the combination of biomarkers can differentiate a responding patient from a non-responding one against the vaccine. 11. The combination of claim 1 , wherein the combination of biomarkers can differentiate a responding patient from a non-responding one against the vaccine before the vaccination. 12. The combination of claim 1 , wherein the combination of biomarkers can differentiate a responding patient from a non-responding one against the vaccine during the vaccination. 13. A method for evaluating efficacy of a vaccine on a patient, the method comprising: a) obtaining a biological sample of each of the patients including the patient from a patient pool including both seroconverted patients and non- seroconverted ones; b) quantifying concentrations of a combination of immuno-biomarkers of claim 1 in the biological sample for each of the patients; c) generating a profile of the immuno-biomarkers from the concentrations of the immuno-biomarkers of each of the patients by using a model; d) determining in the profile whether each of the immuno-biomarkers of each of the patients is expressed or not expressed, wherein one immuno-biomarker of the patient is expressed if its concentration is above a cut-off concentration and otherwise it is unexpressed; e) setting every possible combination of Boolean states as a criterion to distinguish converted patients from non-converted; and f) classifying the patient as a converted one (or a responding one) to the vaccine if the patient's Boolean state consists with the criterion state and as a 

 non-converted one (or a non-responding one) otherwise. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the vaccine is a hepatitis B vaccine. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the vaccine is a chronic hepatitis B vaccine. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the vaccine is an HBsAg-HBIG therapeutic vaccine (YIC). 17. The method of claim 13, wherein the vaccine is a hepatitis B vaccine in combination of GM-CSF. 18. The method of claim 13, wherein the model is a Boolean modeling. 19. The method of claim 13, wherein the cut-off concentration is 0.1 pg/ml. 20. The method of claim 16, wherein the immuno-biomarkers comprises IL-10, IL-33 and MIP-1 a. 21. The method of claim 16, wherein the immuno-biomarkers consists of IL-10, IL-33 and MIP-1 a. 22. The method of claim 17, wherein the immuno-biomarkers either comprise IL- 10, IL-13, IL-23, IFN-a, IL-18 and MIG6 or comprise IL-13, IFN-a, and IL-18. 23. The method of claim 17, wherein the immuno-biomarkers either consist of IL- 10, IL-13, IL-23, IFN-a, IL-18 and MIG6 or consist of IL-13, IFN-a, and IL-18. 24. The method of claim 13, wherein the biological sample is obtained from the patient before vaccination by the vaccine. 25. The method of claim 13, wherein the biological sample is obtained from the patient during vaccination by the vaccine. 26. The method of claim 13, wherein the biological sample comprises 

 cerebrospinal fluid, whole blood, blood serum, plasma, urine, saliva, other bodily fluid, breath, condensed breath, or an extract, purification, or dilution of any of these. 27. The method of claim 13, wherein the quantifying step uses a technique comprising SELDI (-TOF) spectrometry, MALDI (-TOF) spectrometry, a 1-D gel- based analysis, a 2-D gel-based analysis, mass spectrometry (MS), liquid chromatography (LC), reverse phase liquid chromatography (RP-LC), size permeation chromatography, gel filtration chromatography, ion exchange chromatography, affinity chromatography, FPLC, HPLC, UPLC, other LC-based techniques, other LC-MS-based technique, or combinations thereof. 28. The method of claim 13, wherein the quantifying step uses a method comprising an immunological method, a biosensor method, a microanalytical method, a microengineered method, a microseparation method, an immunochromatography method, or combinations thereof. 29. A kit for differentiating a responding patient from a non-responding one against a vaccine, the kit comprising a biosensor capable of detecting and/or quantifying the combination of biomarkers as defined in any of claims 1 to 12.
